items.title: Tata Electronics, a major tech supplier to Apple and Tesla, confirms data breach
items.content: The incident comes as Tata Electronics expands its role in global technology supply chains.

items.title: A new unpatchable flaw in Apple chips opens the door to an iPhone jailbreak
items.content: European offensive cybersecurity company Paradigm Shift released details of a flaw and a technique to exploit it that opens the door for hackers to unlock and break into older

items.title: Beyond Siri: Here are the practical AI features coming to your iPhone in iOS 27
items.content: Siri’s AI overhaul may have grabbed the headlines at WWDC, but some of Apple’s most useful AI features are arriving elsewhere in iOS 27.

items.title: Every new iOS 27 feature that’s worth knowing about
items.content: While it's not flashy like Apple’s new Siri AI and Apple Intelligence upgrades, there are still a number of additions to iOS 27 worth looking at.

items.title: Microsoft and Chevron plan one of the largest gas-powered data center projects in US
items.content: Microsoft inked a 20-year power purchase agreement with Chevron, locking in decades of carbon emissions from a new natural gas power plant.

items.title: Microsoft taps Alt Carbon in sign of India’s growing role in carbon removal
items.content: Alt Carbon said the agreement followed more than a year of scientific review and due diligence, with Microsoft requiring additional verification and data-sharing measures.

items.title: Microsoft’s open source tools were hacked to steal passwords of AI developers
items.content: Microsoft shut down dozens of GitHub code repositories for Azure and AI coding tools after a reported hack.

items.title: Is this the dawn of the Tokenpocalypse?
items.content: We're likely to see more price increases as the big AI companies plan to go public.

items.title: Teamsters Make a Bid to End Federal Oversight, With Trump’s Backing
items.content: Sean M. O’Brien, re-elected to a second term leading the union, has used a relationship with President Trump to end court-ordered corruption monitoring.

items.title: CBS News Hired an Independent Watchdog. What’s He Doing?
items.content: With CBS News rocked by controversy, some viewers have turned to the network ombudsman for a public response. So far, they haven’t gotten one.

items.title: The Iran War’s Persistent Threat to Farmers in Poor Countries
items.content: A reopening of the Strait of Hormuz would do little to swiftly ease the pain inflicted by higher prices for fertilizer, food and fuel in Ivory Coast.

items.title: Ferraris and Shell Companies: Five Charged in Medicare Fraud Schemes
items.content: The Justice Department is taking aim at pricey “skin substitutes,” an overused wound care treatment that cost the government $10 billion in 2024.
